cards.front: What is an N-gram model in Language Modeling?
cards.back: An N-gram model is a statistical model that predicts the next word in a sentence based on a pattern of N words that have come before it.

cards.front: What is an unigram in the N-gram model?
cards.back: An unigram is the simplest type of N-gram model, where each word is considered as a separate unit, and the model predicts the next word based on its individual unigrams.

cards.front: What is a bigram in the N-gram model?
cards.back: A bigram is a type of N-gram model where the model predicts the next word based on the previous word, i.e., it uses a pair of consecutive words to make a prediction.

The Panillion Framework is a linguistic framework that analyzes the structure of French poetry, but it has also been applied to other languages, including the study of linguistic patterns and relationships in language modeling.

cards.front: What is Karaka theory in Language Modeling?
cards.back: Karaka theory is an Arabic linguistic theory that studies the patterns of suffixes and prefixes in Arabic words, and has been applied to the study of linguistic patterns in language modeling, particularly in the context of Arabic language processing.
